title: Task Contract
description: Contract every non-trivial task must satisfy before any edit is made. Authored by reliability-spec-critic, agreed by reliability-lead, checked by reliability-verifier. Prevents early requirements from being silently dropped later in the trajectory.

task_id.description: Stable identifier for this task. Used in evidence-ledger and verification-report references.

base_commit.description: Git commit SHA the task started from. The clean-checkout verifier creates a fresh worktree at this commit and applies the frozen patch.

goal.description: One-sentence statement of what the user actually wants. Written from the user's point of view, not the agent's.

must.description: Observable outcomes the patch MUST produce. Each entry must be checkable by a command or inspection.

must_not.description: Behaviors or side effects the patch MUST NOT introduce. Each entry must be checkable.

non_goals.description: Explicitly out-of-scope items. Listing them prevents scope creep and lets the verifier reject unrelated changes.

acceptance_criteria.description: Structured, individually-checkable criteria. Every MUST maps to at least one acceptance criterion.

id.description: Stable identifier, e.g. AC1, AC2. Referenced by verification-report.acceptance_results[].

condition.description: Observable, unambiguous condition. Must be checkable by a command, test, or inspection — not by vibes.

allowed_scope.description: Glob patterns or paths the patch is allowed to touch. Files outside this set trigger a scope_violation in the verification report.

preserved_invariants.description: Properties that must hold before AND after the patch (e.g., 'public API signature of X unchanged', 'no new dependencies added'). The verifier checks each.

blocking_unknowns.description: Unresolved questions that block confident implementation. If non-empty, the lead must either resolve them with the user or proceed at PARTIALLY_VERIFIED at best.

risk_tier.description: Drives routing (see core/routing.md). standard = main + verifier; complex = orthogonal scouts + lead + verifier; critical = complex + adversary.
